case_title: TASKRABBIT INC. VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION MOTION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE (TRANSACTION ID # 60473227) FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TASKRABBIT, INC.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO MAY 24, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO BRIEF A THRESHOLD ISSUE, NOT RAISED BY THE PARTIES, WHETHER THIS WRIT PETITION RAISES A JUSTICIABLE CONTROVERSY. BASED ON THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, THE COURT TENTATIVELY CONCLUDES THAT THIS WRIT PETITION DOES NOT RAISE A JUSTICIABLE CONTROVERSY AND THUS THE COURT IS OF THE VIEW THAT THE WRIT PETITION SHOULD BE GRANTED AND THE CASE REMANDED TO THE APPEALS BOARD TO VACATE ITS DECISION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO CLAIM FOR IT TO ADJUDICATE. THE APPEALS BOARD MAY FILE A BRIEF ON THIS ISSUE NO LATER THAN MAY 5, 2017 AND TASKRABBIT MAY FILE A RESPONSIVE BRIEF NO LATER MAY 15, 2017. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-24-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Baldwin J. Lee, of Allen Matkins, Tel.415.837.1515, for plaintiffs;
Alex Nestor, of Allen Matkins, Tel.415.837.1515, for plaintiffs;
Hamsa M. Murthy, of Office of the Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5598, for defendant California Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PETITIONER TASKRABBIT INC.'s Motion For Writ Of Mandate is granted. Order signed. (See full Word minutes and/or order for complete ruling). Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Continued To May-24-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Petitioner TaskRabbit, Inc.'s petition for writ of mandate is continued to May 24, 2017 to give the parties an opportunity to brief a threshold issue, not raised by the parties, whether this writ petition raises a justiciable controversy. (See generally 3 Witkin, California Procedure (Fifth Edition 2008) "Actions" sec. 21 et seq.). A review of the administrative record does not disclose that claimant Ms. Gonzalez made a claim for unemployment insurance benefits. Neither the decision of the Employment Development Department which was the subject of the administrative hearing nor the decision of respondent Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board requires TaskRabbit to pay any benefits to Ms. Gonzalez or to do or refrain from doing any action as to Ms. Gonzalez or anyone else. Nor did Ms. Gonzalez file an administrative claim, even assuming such a claim exists, to declare that she was an employee, not an independent contractor, of TaskRabbit. As of the administrative hearing Ms. Gonzalez continued to do "tasks" for clients through the TaskRabbit platform. It appears that at all relevant times Ms. Gonzalez considered herself an independent contractor and never made a claim to EDD otherwise. Put simply, the court tentatively concludes that there was no claim made by Ms. Gonzalez, no claim that could have been administratively adjudicated, the EDD should never have made a determination that Ms. Gonzalez was an employee, the ALJ should never have made a determination that she was not, and the Appeals Board should never have made a determination that she was. Based on the administrative record, the court tentatively concludes that this writ petition does not raise a justiciable controversy and thus the court is of the view that the writ petition should be granted and the case remanded to the Appeals Board to vacate its decision because there was no claim for it to adjudicate. The Appeals Board may file a brief on this issue no later than May 5, 2017 and TaskRabbit may file a responsive brief no later May 15, 2017. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
